topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias traced the ink on the aged parchment, his knuckles white against the brittle paper. The dates, the achievements, the even the seemingly trivial anecdotes—they all pointed back to a life he barely recognized. A cold knot tightened in his chest, a tight, resistant pressure that made his breath catch. He reread the passage about his early travels, the descriptions vivid and detailed, yet utterly alien. "This is absurd," he muttered, the sound rough in the quiet study. He pushed the document away, the movement sharp and decisive. He wouldn't accept this fabrication. He straightened his spine, the muscles in his neck corded with a refusal to be moved, and began methodically searching for the source, his gaze sharp and unwavering.

[story 2]

"Impossible," Clara whispered, her voice trembling slightly as she stared at the meticulously crafted narrative laid out before her. Every sentence felt like a carefully placed trap. She clenched her jaw so tightly a dull ache radiated across her temples. She didn't immediately destroy the manuscript; instead, she ran her fingers over the elegant script, feeling the slickness of the deception. A deep, simmering resentment began to bubble up, hot and slow. She stood up, pacing the length of the room, her movements jerky with contained agitation. "Someone has manufactured this," she stated to the empty air, her tone flat but edged with steel. She would find the architect of this lie, no matter how deep the trail led.

[story 3]

When the solicitor presented the finalized biography, Marcus simply folded the document with a deliberate, slow motion. He didn't look up as he spoke, his voice low and steady. "It is what it is, Mr. Vance." Marcus felt a sudden, profound stillness settle over him, a hardening in his core that made his hands grip the edge of the mahogany desk. He felt a fierce, almost physical refusal to concede the reality presented. He leaned back slowly, deliberately, forcing a calm façade onto his features, though the tension radiating from him was palpable. "I will require a full accounting of the process," he managed, his voice level, refusing to let the tremor in his hands betray the intensity of his resolve.
